title: Sorbus betulaefolia
taxonomy: Sorbus betulaefolia is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Rosaceae. It is a member of the genus Sorbus, commonly known as rowans, which comprises around 100 species of deciduous trees and shrubs. The plant is native to the mountainous regions of China and is often found growing at high elevations. Sorbus betulaefolia is known to be a rare and endangered species due to habitat loss and other environmental factors. The plant's classification is further broken down into the kingdom Plantae and the division Magnoliophyta.
uses: Sorbus betulaefolia, also known as the Chinese rowan or Chinese mountain ash, has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant's bark, leaves, and seeds are used to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and respiratory issues. In traditional Chinese medicine, Sorbus betulaefolia is believed to have a balancing effect on the body's energies, or "qi." Its medicinal uses also extend to treating digestive problems and skin conditions. However, more research is needed to confirm the plant's efficacy and safety for human consumption.
